questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: Study the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
Arrange the following words as per order in the dictionary.

1. Command    2. Commit    3. Connect    4. Conceive    5. Conduct    6. Commerce
questions.solution:
Command 
(1)		 Commit 
(2)		 Connect 
(3)		 Conceive 
(4) 		 Conduct 
(5)		 Commerce 
(6)
↓↓↓↓↓↓
   After removing the common letter "Co" from the words   
↓↓↓↓↓↓
mmandmmitnnectnceivenductmmerce
↓↓↓↓↓↓
                     Now, we can find out the order                      
↓↓↓↓↓↓
1st3rd6 th4th5th2nd

So, the order as per the given words is: 1, 6, 2, 4, 5, 3.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Which one of the given responses would be a meaningful order of the following?

1. Chaudhary Charan Singh

2. Morarji Desai

3. Indira Gandhi

4. Jawaharlal Nehru

5. Lal Bahadur Shastri
questions.solution:
Prime ministers of India are arranged in chronological order.

4. Jawaharlal Nehru

5. Lal Bahadur Shastri

3. Indira Gandhi

2. Morarji Desai

1. Chaudhary Charan Singh

Hence, the correct sequence is "4, 5, 3, 2, 1"
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If all the letters of the word ‘CHEVROLET’ that come before ‘M’ in English alphabet series are changed to their second next letter as per alphabet series and all the letters of the same word that come after ‘M’ in English alphabet series are changed to their reverse letter then find the fifth letter from left end of the word so formed?
questions.solution:
Given word: CHEVROLET
 
New word: EJGEILNGG
 
Thus fifth letter from left end is ‘I’.
 
Hence option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the given words in a logical and meaningful order.

1. Starfish

2. Blue Whale

3. Shark

4. Giant Tortoise

5. penguin
questions.solution:
The logical and meaningful order of the given words is as follows:

LOGIC: The organisms have been arranged according to their size

1. Starfish

5. Penguin

4. Giant Tortoise

3. Shark

2. Blue Whale

Hence, "1, 5, 4, 3, 2" is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
What is the third letter of the meaningful English word which is formed using 3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th and 7th letters of the word “COMPARISON”? (Each letter to be used once)If more than one word can be formed then mark “X” as your answer, if no meaningful English word can be formed then mark “Y” as your answer.
questions.solution:
3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th and 7th letters of COMPARISON are M,P,A,R and I.
 
The meaningful English word formed so is PRIMA which means first.
 
The third letter of it is I.
 
Hence option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If it is possible to make a meaningful word, using the 2nd, 4th, 5th and 9th letters (counting from left to right) of the word 'DEDUCTION', then what will be the 1st letter from left of the word so formed? Mark 'Y' as your answer if no such word can be formed. If more than one word can be formed mark the answer as 'X'.
questions.solution:
The 2nd, 4th, 5th and 9th letters in the given word are E, U, C and N respectively but no meaningful word can be formed using the letters.
 
Option D is hence the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.solution:
Meaningful word ⇒ B E A C H
       ↓
        Fourth
       Letter

Hence, option B is correct.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the given words in a logical and meaningful order.

1. Eyes

2. Nose

3. Fingers

4. Hair

5. Bones
questions.solution:
The logic followed here is:

The meaningful order is according on the basis of their number.

2. Nose \(\rightarrow\) (Human have only one nose)

1. Eyes \(\rightarrow\) (Human have only two eyes)

3. Fingers \(\rightarrow\) (Human have 10 fingers)

5. Bones \(\rightarrow\) (Human have 206 Bones in their body)

4. Hair \(\rightarrow\) (Human have innumerable number of hair in their body)

Hence, the correct answer is "2,1,3,5,4"
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Arrange the following words in a logical and meaningful order

1. Colonel

2. Field Marshal

3. Brigadier

4. Lieutenant

5. General
questions.solution:
These are the Ranks in an Army arranged in descending order.

2. Field Marshal

5. General

3. Brigadier

1. Colonel

4. Lieutenant

Hence, 2, 5, 3, 1, 4 is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
What will be the last letter of the meaningful 4-letter English word formed using 1st and 5th letter of “Barber” along with 2nd and 6th letter of “Parking” such that each letter to be used once? If more than one such word can be formed then mark ‘X’ as your answer, if no such word can be formed then mark “Y’ as your answer.
questions.solution:
1st and 5th letters of Barber are B and E.

2nd and 6th letters of Parking are A and N.
 
Using all 4 letters together we can make 2 meaningful English words – BEAN (an edible seed) and BANE (cause of distress).

Hence option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Comprehension:

In each of the following questions arrange the given words in meaningful sequence and choose the correct sequence from the given alternatives.

1. Birth 2. Death 3. Funeral 4. Marriage 5. Education
questions.solution:
The correct order of words in a logical and meaningful order is:

1. Birth \(\rightarrow\) Birth is the beginning of a person's life. It marks the entry of an individual into the world, so it is the first event in this sequence.

5. Education \(\rightarrow\) Education generally follows birth and begins shortly after a child is born. Education continues throughout childhood and early adulthood, preparing them for various roles in life.

4. Marriage \(\rightarrow\) Marriage typically occurs after reaching adulthood, following a period of education and personal growth. After education, individuals may get married, forming a family.

2. Death \(\rightarrow\) Death is the end of life, generally happening after marriage and other life events like work, family, etc.

3. Funeral \(\rightarrow\) A funeral is the event that occurs after death to honor and commemorate the deceased.

The correct order is '1,5,4,2,3'.

Hence, the correct answer is "Option 3".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Arrange the following words in a logical and meaningful order.

2. Mesosphere

3. Ionosphere

6. Exosphere
questions.solution:
The logical and meaningful order of the given words is: 5. Troposphere

1. Stratosphere

2. Mesosphere

4. Thermosphere

3. Ionosphere

6. Exosphere

These are the layers of the atmosphere arranged according to their actual position.

Hence, the correct answer is option (4).

questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If all the consonants before 'M' (in alphabet series) of the word "CONSIDERATION" are changed to their immediately succeeding letters (as per alphabet series) and all the consonants after 'M' (in alphabet series) of the same word are changed to their immediately preceding letters (as per alphabet series) then what is the exactly middle letter of the meaningful english (singular) word formed from third, fourth, sixth, ninth and tenth letters of the changed word?
questions.solution:
Given word  :    CONSIDERATION
 
Changed Word:DOMRIEEQASIOM 
 
Third, Fourth, Sixth, Ninth nd Tenth letters are M,R,E,A and S.
 
Meaningful english (singular) word formed is SMEAR, which means to put false allegations on someone to damage his/her reputation.
 
Exactly middle letter is 'E'.
 
Hence option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

If in the word "IRONICAL" all the vowels are changed to their second next letter as per English alphabet series and then all the letters are arranged as per reverse alphabetical order then how many letters in English alphabet series are between the third letter from right end and fifth letter from left end?
questions.solution:
Given word: IRONICAL

New word: KRQNKCCL 

Arranging letters: RQNLKKCC

Third letter from right end is K and fifth letter from left end is also K.

Hence option D is correct.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: Which one of the given responses would be a meaningful order of the following?

l. Vice-President

2. President

3. Speaker

4. Prime minister

5. Member of Parliament
questions.solution:
In political hierarchy,

President comes first.

President is followed by Vice-President.

Vice-President is followed by Prime minister.

The Prime minister is followed by Speaker.

Speaker is followed by a Member of Parliament.

The correct logical order is,

2) President \(\rightarrow\) 1) Vice-President \(\rightarrow\) 4) Prime minister \(\rightarrow\) 3) Speaker \(\rightarrow\) 5) Member of Parliament

Hence, "2, 1, 4, 3, 5" is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If all the letters at even numbered position from left end in the word “AVENGERS” are changed to their previous letter as per English alphabet series then which of the following meaningful words can be formed using 2nd, 3rd, 6th and 8th letters of the new word?

I. RUDE
II. RAGE
III. DURE
questions.solution:
Word given: AVENGERS

New word : AUEMGDRR

2nd, 3rd, 6th and 8th letters of the new word are U,E,D and R.

Two meaningful words are RUDE and DURE which means Harsh and Hard respectively.

Thus only I and III represent such words.

Hence option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If a meaningful English word has to be formed using 1st, 4th, 6th, 8th and 9th letters of the word (each letter to be used only once) ‘PLASMODIUM’ then which of the following will be the 4th letter from the right end? If more than one such word can be formed then mark ‘X’ as your answer and if no meaningful word can be formed then mark ‘Z’ as your answer?
questions.solution:
The given word: P L A S M O D I U M

1st, 4th, 6th, 8th and 9th letters are P, S, O, I and U respectively.

The only word which can be foremd with these letters is PIOUS

And the 4th letter from the right end is I.

 Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
How many meaning full English words can be formed using the second, third, fifth and seventh letters of the word ‘KITCHEN’ using each letter once in each word?
questions.solution:
Second, third, fifth and seventh letters of the word are I, T, H and N respectively.
 
The words formed from the letters = Hint and Thin
 
Hence, two words can be formed from the given letters above.
 
Hence, the correct answer is option C.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
How many meaningful words can be made by using letters ‘L’, ‘O’, ‘S’ and ‘U’, keeping S as the first letter of the word?
questions.solution:
Words that can be formed using O, U, S and L while keeping S as the first letter: SOUL
 
Clearly, option B is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If all the letters in the given word that come after 'M' in English alphabet series are either immediately preceded or immediately succeeded by a vowel then change such letter to its reverse letter as per the alphabet series then how many meaningful english words can be formed from the first, second, seventh and ninth letter of the newly formed word?

PROPHYLACTIC
questions.solution:
Given Word: PROPHYLACTIC

New Word: PIOKHYLACGIC

Its first, second, seventh and ninth letters are: P, I, L and C.

Meaningful word using these letters is "CLIP", which is a device used for holding objects.

Hence option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If the letters in the word 'CREATION' are rearranged as they appear in the English alphabet then the position of how many letters will remain unchanged after the rearrangement?
questions.solution:
The given letter
 
C R E A T I O N
 
After arranging the letters of the given word as they appear in the English alphabet, we have:
 
A C E I N O R T
 
Only 'E' remains at the same position after rearrangement.
 
Hence, option 'B' is the Correct choice.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If it is possible to make to make only one meaningful word with the first, the third, the fifth and the ninth letters of the word 'FRAGRANCE', which of the following will be second letter of the word? If no such word can be made, give 'Y' as the answer and if more than one such words can be made give 'Z' as the answer.
questions.solution:
The given word

FRAGRANCE
1st2nd3rd4th5th6th7th8th9th
 
The  first, the third, the fifth and the ninth letters of the above given the word are –

F, A, R, E
 
while rearranging these letters only once, two meaningful words can be formed –
 
FARE, FEAR.
 
Thus, option C is the right answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If a meaningful English word has to be formed using second, fifth, sixth and ninth letters of the word PREMATURE then which is the third letter of the new word. If no such word can be formed then mark ‘X’ as your answer and if more than one word can be formed then mark ‘Y’ as your answer.
questions.solution:
Second, fifth, sixth and ninth letters of the word PREMATURE are R,A,T and E.
 
Meaningful English words are RATE, TEAR and TARE.
 
Thus more than one word is formed, thus answer should be Y .
 
Hence option E is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
Which of the following is the third letter of the meaningful English word which can be formed using (each letter once) first, third, fourth, sixth, eighth and ninth letters of “ASTRONAUT”?
questions.solution:
First, third, fourth, sixth, eighth and ninth letters of “ASTRONAUT” are – A,T,R,N,U and T.
 
Truant is the only meaningful English word which can be formed using the above letters.
 
‘U’ is the third letter of the word Truant.
 
Hence option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
Each consonant of the word ‘TERMINATION’ is changed to the previous letter in the English alphabetical series and each vowel is changed to the next letter in the English alphabetical series. If the new alphabets thus formed are arranged in alphabetical order (from left to right), which of the following will be the sixth letter from the right end?
questions.solution:
The given word:

T  E  R  M  I  N  A  T   I  O  N

Applying the above condition, we have new word:

S  F  Q  L  J  M  B  S  J  P  M

Now, arranging in alphabetical order (from left to right)

B  F J  J  L  M  M  P  Q  S  S

Hence, M is sixth from the right.

Hence, the correct answer is option A.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions : Read the following questions carefully and choose the right answer.
If the letters in the word ‘MOTHERLAND’ rearranged as they appear in the English alphabet then the position of how many letter will remain unchanged after the rearrangement?
questions.solution:
The given letter:

M O T H E R L A N D

After, arranging the letters of the given word as they appear in the English alphabet, we have:

A D E H L M N O R T

Observing the above arrangement, we can clearly see that its H whose position remain unchanged.

Hence, the correct answer is option A.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
